mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines across a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, compressing corporate margins and pressuring equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE sustains values below 5.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion combined with rising industrial production signals synchronized demand-pull inflation and capacity utilization, driving equity premiums higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and INDPRO fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising inflation environment, real interest rates compress as nominal policy rates lag price increases, temporarily boosting equity valuations until the central bank tightens aggressively.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains flat, contradicting the real rate compression premise.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect improving consumer confidence and credit availability, leading to positive wealth effects that lift broad equity indices over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no monotonic relationship with HOUST levels across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth decelerates below its six-month moving average, supply chain bottlenecks or demand weakness emerge, triggering a negative equity return signal over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite sustained INDPRO deceleration below the 0.5% threshold for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth coupled with contracting consumer credit indicates unsustainable consumption financed by debt, leading to a mean-reverting equity pullback over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence between rising PAYEMS and falling RSAFS does not precede negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating real GDP growth, corporate earnings revisions turn positive, driving equity outperformance over a one-month window as fundamental valuations expand.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when GDPC1 growth accelerates above 2.5% annualized, invalidating the earnings revision channel.
